name: Puerto de Tornavacas – running on the Camino Natural del Tajo (GR 10)
name_original: Puerto de Tornavacas – running por el Camino Natural del Tajo (GR 10)
region_intro: Jogging routes around Tornavacas are situated within the Jerte Valley, at the base of the Gredos and Béjar mountain ranges. The region features mountainous terrain with varied slopes, numerous gorges, and the Jerte River. A significant portion of the area is part of the Garganta de los Infiernos Natural Reserve, offering diverse landscapes for running. This environment provides a range of running experiences, from paths along river valleys to more challenging mountain trails through cherry, oak, and chestnut forests. Best jogging routes around Tornavacas The most popular jogging route is Canchal de la Ceja y El Torreón - circular trail run in the Sierra de Candelario Natural Space, an 8.7 miles (13.9 km) trail that takes 2 hours 38 minutes to complete. This route offers challenging climbs within a natural space. Another top favourite among local runners is Ruta del Trabuquete – running circular by the Old Path of Castilla, a difficult 6.9 miles (11.1 km) path. This route follows historical paths with significant elevation changes. Local runners also love the Lagunas del Trampal por el Canchal de la Ceja – circular by the Espacio Natural de la Sierra de Candelario y Parque Regional de la Sierra de Gredos, an 8.9 miles (14.3 km) trail leading through mountain landscapes and natural parks, often completed in about 2 hours 45 minutes. Jogging around Tornavacas is defined by mountainous terrain, the Jerte Valley, and numerous gorges. The network offers options for various ability levels, from moderate paths to difficult mountain trails. The routes in Tornavacas are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from 1 review. More than 300 runners have used komoot to explore Tornavacas's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available around Tornavacas?
A: There are over 50 running routes available around Tornavacas, offering a wide range of experiences for different skill levels. You'll find everything from moderate paths to challenging mountain trails.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on running trails in Tornavacas?
A: Running in Tornavacas means navigating diverse terrain within the Jerte Valley, nestled between the Gredos and Béjar mountain ranges. Expect mountainous landscapes with varied slopes, numerous gorges, and paths alongside the Jerte River. Many routes pass through cherry, oak, and chestnut forests, and a significant portion of the area is part of the Garganta de los Infiernos Natural Reserve, known for its unique rock formations and waterfalls.

Q: Are there any easy or beginner-friendly running routes in Tornavacas?
A: While many routes in Tornavacas are challenging, there is at least one easy running route available. The region offers a variety of paths, so it's possible to find less strenuous options, particularly along river valleys, though most trails feature significant elevation changes.

Q: Can I find challenging trail running routes in Tornavacas?
A: Absolutely. Tornavacas is ideal for those seeking challenging trail running experiences. With 35 difficult routes out of 50, you'll encounter steep slopes and mountain terrain, reaching up to 2,400 meters in the Calviterio mountains. Routes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e993406094/canchal-de-la-ceja-y-el-torreon-circular-trail-run-in-the-sierra-de-candelario-natural-space" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Canchal de la Ceja y El Torreón - circular trail run&lt;/a&gt; offer significant climbs and rewarding views.

Q: Are there any circular running routes in Tornavacas?
A: Yes, many of the running routes in Tornavacas are circular, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e990911001/ruta-del-trabuquete-running-circular-by-the-old-path-of-castilla" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ruta del Trabuquete – running circular by the Old Path of Castilla&lt;/a&gt; is a popular difficult circular path following historical trails.

Q: What natural attractions can I see along the running routes in Tornavacas?
A: The running routes in Tornavacas offer access to stunning natural attractions. You can explore the famous 'Los Pilones' in the Garganta de los Infiernos Natural Reserve, known for its natural pools and unique rock formations. Other highlights include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/874276" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Charco del Trabuquete&lt;/a&gt; waterfall and various viewpoints like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5638307" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Chorrero de la Virgen Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt;, offering panoramic vistas of the Jerte Valley and surrounding mountains.

Q: Are there any running routes that pass by lakes or natural pools?
A: Yes, several routes in the Tornavacas area lead to or pass by beautiful natural pools and lakes. Beyond Los Pilones, you can find natural pools like El Charco del Trabuquete. The region also features lakes such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2653780" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Laguna de los Caballeros&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2653623" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Laguna del Duque&lt;/a&gt;, which can be destinations for longer runs, particularly in warmer months.

Q: What do other runners enjoy most about jogging in Tornavacas?
A: The running routes around Tornavacas are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars. Runners frequently praise the diverse terrain, the stunning natural beauty of the Jerte Valley, and the challenging mountain trails that offer rewarding views. The area's combination of river paths, forests, and high-altitude sections makes for a varied and invigorating experience.

Q: Are there any historical or cultural points of interest along the running trails?
A: Yes, you can incorporate historical elements into your runs. Tornavacas itself has traditional architecture, medieval bridges like Puente Cimero, and ancient hermitages. Some routes, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e990911001/ruta-del-trabuquete-running-circular-by-the-old-path-of-castilla" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ruta del Trabuquete&lt;/a&gt;, follow historical paths, offering a glimpse into the local heritage while you run.

Q: What is the best time of year to go running in Tornavacas?
A: Tornavacas offers great running opportunities year-round, but the spring, especially during the cherry blossom season in the Valle del Jerte, is particularly stunning. Autumn also provides beautiful scenery with changing leaf colors. Summer can be warm, making routes with natural pools like El Charco del Trabuquete refreshing, while winter offers a different, often more challenging, mountain running experience.

Q: Are there any long-distance running routes or trails that pass through Tornavacas?
A: Yes, Tornavacas is a hub for several marked trails, including sections of long-distance routes. The GR-10 route (Puerto de Tornavacas-Puerto de Honduras) passes through the area, offering extensive options for longer runs. You can experience a section of this on the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e991218878/puerto-de-tornavacas-running-on-the-camino-natural-del-tajo-gr-10" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerto de Tornavacas – running on the Camino Natural del Tajo (GR 10)&lt;/a&gt; route.
tips: Located at 1,275 meters above sea level, it is recognized as a historic mountain pass in the Sierra de Gredos and represents the physical division point of the Duero and Tagus basins. This site has been the passage of several civilizations. over the centuries and represents a site of great historical importance for the area.

waypoints.name: Viewpoint of the Puerto de Tornavacas
